mission.title: vllm: Support for grammar
mission.description: Support for grammar. Requested by 25+ users.

steps.title: Check current vllm setup
steps.description:
Verify your vllm version and configuration:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
```
This feature requires a working vllm installation.

steps.title: Review vllm configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vllm configuration:
It would be highly beneficial if the library could incorporate support for Grammar and GBNF files.
https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/grammars/README.md

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for grammar
steps.description:
Hi! Sorry for the delay. I ended up implementing a minor speedup for constrained generation on llama.cpp by skipping "obvious" tokens and just decoding the ones that _need_ an LLM. Although it managed to reach around double the original decoding performance, it is still not enough for my use case, so I will likely revisit this approach with vLLM in the future. 
It is not the current priority for my personal project though, so it may take some weeks before I start experimenting again. 

If

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support for grammar" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Hi! Sorry for the delay. I ended up implementing a minor speedup for constrained generation on llama.cpp by skipping "obvious" tokens and just decoding the ones that _need_ an LLM. Although it managed to reach around double the original decoding performance, it is still not enough for my use case, so I will likely revisit this approach with vLLM in the future.

prerequisites.description: A working vllm installation or development environment.
